NEW DELHI: New Zealand great Kane Williamson on Friday announced his retirement from international cricket, bringing the curtain down on one of the most distinguished careers in the country's cricketing history.The 35-year-old's decision marks the end of an era for New Zealand cricket, with Williamson stepping away from all international formats after more than 15 years at the highest level. The announcement comes a year after he had already decided to move on from T20 Internationals.Widely regarded as one of the finest batters of his generation, Williamson leaves the game as New Zealand's highest run-scorer in international cricket. Across formats, he amassed 19,346 runs and established himself as the backbone of the Black Caps batting line-up for well over a decade.His greatest success came in Test cricket, where he scored more than 9,500 runs, including 33 centuries and six double hundreds.
Article Summary: Kane Williamson has announced his retirement from international cricket. The 35-year-old has played for New Zealand for more than 15 years. Williamson is regarded as one of the finest run-scorer in New Zealand cricket history. He scored more than 9,500 runs in Test cricket, including 33 centuries and six double centuries.

Article Title: Kylian Mbappe caught in Ibiza love storm as his bikini-clad girlfriend Ester Exposito fuels romance speculation ahead of FIFA World Cup 2026
Article Text:
Kylian Mbappe caught in Ibiza love storm as his bikini-clad girlfriend Ester Exposito fuels romance speculation ahead of FIFA World Cup 2026 (via G3/The Grosby Group)

Kylian Mbappe and Ester Exposito’s Ibiza escape turns heads



As the FIFA World Cup 2026 begins, France star Kylian Mbappe has found himself making headlines away from the football field. The Real Madrid striker was recently photographed enjoying a luxury vacation in Ibiza with Spanish actress Ester Exposito. The images, published this month but reportedly taken in May, have reignited speculation that the two are romantically involved. Neither Mbappe nor Exposito has publicly commented on the nature of their relationship.The photos quickly went viral on social media. They show the pair spending time together aboard a luxury yacht in the Mediterranean. Mbappe and Exposito appeared relaxed and comfortable in each other’s company. From sunbathing on deck to sharing affectionate moments and enjoying water activities, the getaway has become one of the most talked-about celebrity stories ahead of the World Cup. The timing of the trip has also drawn fresh attention from football fans as Mbappe prepares to lead France’s quest for another world title.The photographs captured several memorable moments from the couple’s day at sea. Kylian Mbappe and Ester Exposito were seen riding jet skis across the Mediterranean waters.The French forward was driving while the actress held onto him as they laughed together. They also spent time paddleboarding. At one point, Exposito fell into the water but appeared to take the incident lightly and laughed as she climbed back up.For the outing, Exposito wore an orange bikini paired with stylish beach accessories. Mbappe chose colorful swim shorts for the yacht trip. Later, the pair were photographed walking barefoot along the beach while carrying their sandals. They eventually stopped at a waterfront restaurant for lunch.As the day continued, Exposito added a green cover-up dress and sunglasses to complete her beach look. Mbappe changed into a black sleeveless shirt, black shorts, a cap, and sunglasses. The football star appeared to be trying to keep a low profile, although the cameras followed their every move.The Ibiza vacation came shortly after the pair were spotted together in Sardinia, Italy. During that earlier trip, they reportedly stayed at a luxury hotel and spent time aboard another yacht. The visit happened while Mbappe was recovering from a hamstring injury.That trip attracted criticism from some Real Madrid supporters, who questioned whether the timing was appropriate given his injury concerns and the club’s disappointing finish to the season. However, people close to the player dismissed those concerns. They explained that his recovery process remained under the supervision of Real Madrid’s medical team and stressed that his dedication to training and helping the club had never been in doubt.
Article Summary: Kylian Mbappe and Ester Exposito were spotted on a luxury yacht in Ibiza. The Real Madrid striker and Spanish actress have been linked to each other in the past. The photos have reignited speculation that the two are romantically involved.

Article Title: USA vs Paraguay: USMNT ends 20-year wait and makes World Cup history as 3rd-fastest host goal in opener
Article Text:
USA opened their 2026 World Cup campaign with a 4-1 win over Paraguay, scoring through a seventh-minute own goal forced by Christian Pulisic’s cross and Weston McKennie’s pressure/ Image:X

How the record-setting goal happened



The fastest goals by host nations in their opening World Cup matches



France vs Belgium, 1938 – 1st minute Germany vs Costa Rica, 2006 – 6th minute (Philipp Lahm) United States vs Paraguay , 2026 – 7th minute (Damián Bobadilla own goal) Mexico vs South Africa, 2026 – 9th minute (Julián Quiñones)

Mexico also enters the history books



USMNT turn historic start into statement victory



A different USMNT under Pochettino



USA could hardly have asked for a better start to their FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign, taking the lead against Paraguay inside the opening minutes in Los Angeles. The breakthrough not only set the hosts on their way to a 4-1 victory at SoFi Stadium but also became the third-fastest goal scored by a host nation in its opening World Cup match, ending a 20-year wait for such a rapid start.Only France in 1938 and Germany in 2006 have found the net earlier in their opening World Cup fixtures as hosts.The breakthrough arrived in the seventh minute as the United States immediately imposed themselves on Paraguay in front of a sold-out crowd of 70,492 in Los Angeles.Weston McKennie delivered a dangerous ball into the penalty area, forcing Paraguay defender Damián Bobadilla into a mistake. Under pressure, Bobadilla diverted the ball into his own net to hand the hosts an early lead.While the goal officially went down as an own goal rather than a strike by an American player, it still counts in the historical record as the goal that gave the United States its opening-match advantage.That seventh-minute breakthrough immediately placed the Americans in rare World Cup company.Only two host nations have scored earlier in their first match of a World Cup:France's first-minute goal against Belgium at the 1938 World Cup remains the fastest ever scored by a host nation in its opening fixture.Germany's famous opener against Costa Rica in 2006, scored by Philipp Lahm in the sixth minute, had stood as the modern benchmark for two decades.The United States did not surpass either record, but their seventh-minute breakthrough became the quickest by a host nation in an opening match since Germany's strike 20 years earlier.The United States were not the only co-hosts to feature on the list. Earlier in the tournament, Mexico's Julián Quiñones had opened the scoring in the ninth minute of El Tri's 2-0 victory over South Africa at Estadio Azteca.That goal now ranks fourth on the all-time list of fastest goals scored by host nations in their opening World Cup matches.The fact that both the United States and Mexico entered the top four on the same opening weekend followed impressive opening victories by two of the tournament's three host nations.The early goal settled any nerves and became the platform for one of the most impressive World Cup performances of Mauricio Pochettino's tenure.Folarin Balogun doubled the lead in the 31st minute after being picked out by Christian Pulisic before adding his second goal in first-half stoppage time to send the United States into the break with a commanding 3-0 advantage.The brace also earned Balogun a place in American football history, making him the first United States player to score multiple goals in a World Cup match since 1930.Paraguay briefly threatened a comeback when substitute Maurício pulled a goal back in the 73rd minute, but any hopes of a late recovery were extinguished deep into stoppage time when Gio Reyna produced a superb outside-of-the-boot finish in the 98th minute to complete a 4-1 victory.The result also offered further evidence of the attacking progress made by the United States under Pochettino. At the 2022 World Cup in Qatar, the Americans managed just three goals across four matches.Against Paraguay, they surpassed that tally in a single evening.For a team carrying the expectations of a home World Cup, the seventh-minute breakthrough may ultimately be remembered for more than its place in the record books. It immediately transformed the atmosphere inside SoFi Stadium and set the tone for a statement victory that launched the United States' campaign in style.Twenty years after Germany's lightning start in 2006, another host nation has written its name into World Cup history.
Article Summary: USA opened their 2026 World Cup campaign with a 4-1 win over Paraguay. The hosts scored through a seventh-minute own goal. It became the third-fastest goal scored by a host nation in its opening World Cup match. Only France in 1938 and Germany in 2006 have found the net earlier.

Article Title: 'I would play entire WC without scoring if France win the trophy': Kylian Mbappe on his FIFA Word Cup 2026 ambitions
Article Text:
In an interview, Kylian Mbappe revealed that for him, the ultimate goal is lifting the World Cup, even if it means sacrificing individual scoring milestones. (Photo/AP)

France star Kylian Mbappe has underlined the importance of collective success over personal achievements, saying he would happily go through an entire FIFA World Cup without scoring a single goal if it means lifting the trophy with France.The 26-year-old French captain, speaking to television channel M6, left no room for complexity when asked whether he'd trade his goal tally for the ultimate prize. "I'd do it in a heartbeat. I'd sign up for that immediately. I'd be the first person celebrating on the Champs-Élysées," Mbappe said.The statement carries weight coming from a player who has already netted 12 goals across two World Cups, a tally that puts him within touching distance of becoming the tournament's all-time leading scorer. Yet the Real Madrid forward insists personal milestones can wait."I've been fortunate enough to play in two World Cups, go far in both, and perform well. Of course, I want to keep making history, but above all I want to return to France with the World Cup trophy," he said.Mbappe knows the agony of near-misses. He was part of the French side that triumphed in Russia in 2018, but endured heartbreak in Qatar 2022 when Les Bleus fell to Argentina on penalties in a pulsating final where he scored a sensational hat-trick.That experience, he says, has only sharpened his hunger."I know how difficult it is to win a World Cup. As the years pass, you have to maintain the same hunger, motivation and excitement. Those are essential qualities if you want to go deep in the tournament," the 27-year-old said.Beyond his own ambitions, Mbappe spoke about taking on a leadership role within the squad, particularly in mentoring younger players through the mental grind of a World Cup. "I'm happy to be here and to support the younger players, especially on the emotional side. Looking at the quality we have in the squad, I don't need to teach anyone how to play football," he added.The skipper also issued a stern reminder about the ruthless nature of knockout football. "A World Cup isn't played over two legs. One bad game can send you home. We have to stay in control of our emotions. That's a message I try to repeat every day because we haven't achieved anything yet," Mbappe said during the interview.France open their World Cup campaign against African powerhouse Senegal on June 17 in their Group I encounter, the group which also includes Norway and Iraq is termed as the ‘group of death’ in this year’s edition.
Article Summary: Kylian Mbappe says he would happily go through an entire FIFA World Cup without scoring a single goal if it means lifting the trophy with France. The 26-year- old French captain, speaking to television channel M6, left no room for complexity when asked whether he'd trade his goal tally for the ultimate prize. He has already netted 12 goals across two World Cups.

Article Title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Thomas Partey's Visa denied; Doubtful for Ghana's World Cup opener against Panama
Article Text:
FIFA WC: Ghana midfielder Partey to miss campaign opener after visa application to enter Canada refused

Ghana’s midfielder Thomas Partey's participation in the side’s FIFA World Cup opener looks doubtful, with his Visa application to Canada being denied. The team will take the field against Panama in Toronto on Wednesday in its opening game."FIFA can confirm that player Thomas Partey will be unable to travel from Ghana's Team Base Camp in Boston, USA, to Canada for their first match against Panama on Wednesday, 17 June, as his visa application has been refused by the Canadian government," FIFA said.“FIFA is not involved in the immigration processes of host countries, including the adjudication of visas. As with previous FIFA events, the host government ultimately determines who receives a visa and is admitted into the country,” FIFA added.The Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada(IRCC) said in a statement that anyone intending to travel to Canada is assessed individually, “based on the facts available and the law that applies”.“Canada is proud to be a host country for the 2026 FIFA World Cup and is working to facilitate a successful event while maintaining the safety and security of Canadians,” the IRCC said in the statement.“Canada has been consistent that hosting major events does not change Canada’s immigration laws,” the IRCC added.Partey remains in the base camp with the Ghana team. The midfielder is, however, eligible to play in the later group stage for his side. Ghana will feature in Group L this FIFA World Cup. The team is set to go against England in Foxborough on 24th June and Croatia in Philadelphia on June 28. Partey has made 7 appearances in the CAF World Cup qualifiers in 2026, scoring 3 goals.The 32-year-old is known for his previous association with Arsenal from 2020 to 2025, the club bought him for 50 Million Euros. The Ghana footballer now features for Villarreal. He is also facing allegations of rape and sexual assault by four different women in the United Kingdom, who have accused him of incidents between 2020 and 2022, but he denies the charges.While legal proceedings continue, Partey remains out on bail, on the condition that he avoids contact with the alleged victims.
Article Summary: Ghana midfielder Thomas Partey's visa application to Canada has been refused. The 32-year-old will miss Ghana's World Cup opener against Panama on Wednesday. Partey remains in the base camp with the Ghana team. The midfielder is eligible to play in the later group stage for his side.

Article Title: England hit by unusual World Cup setback after equipment theft en route to Kansas City
Article Text:
England squad. Image via: Kirsty Wigglesworth/ AP

What was stolen from England's World Cup team in Kansas City?



Detail



Info



Theft location



Transit vehicles en route to Kansas City, MO



Items stolen



Match boots, tournament balls, training equipment



Police response



2 subjects of interest arrested, investigation ongoing



First training session



Sunday





How could the England kit theft affect Thomas Tuchel's preparations for Croatia?



England's Football Association reported a theft of training equipment, including match boots for multiple players and tournament balls, from team vehicles travelling from Palm Beach Gardens, Florida to Swope Soccer Village in Kansas City, Missouri on Friday, June 13, 2026. The Kansas City Missouri Police Department confirmed two subjects of interest were taken into custody pending further investigation. England, managed by Thomas Tuchel, open their 2026 FIFA World Cup Group L campaign against Croatia in Dallas on Wednesday at 21:00 BST.The FA's logistical team had transferred equipment from the squad's pre-tournament training base in Palm Beach Gardens, Florida, to their new base at Swope Soccer Village in Kansas City ahead of Thomas Tuchel and the players' arrival on Saturday afternoon. When the vehicles were unloaded, items were missing. Sources told ESPN that the training kit was among what had been taken.The Guardian reported the stolen items included match boots for several of England's first-team players, tournament balls, and general training equipment. The FA confirmed it was working to determine exactly what had been taken. The Kansas City Missouri Police Department issued a formal statement: "We are investigating a possible theft of equipment from a team vehicle that arrived in Kansas City with items missing this evening.The investigation is ongoing." Within minutes, police confirmed: "Two subjects of interest were taken into custody pending further investigation." Officers were on site Friday night and remained in contact with FA representatives throughout.England's players were given Saturday off before flying to Kansas City, with the squad's first full training session at Swope Soccer Village scheduled for Sunday. That leaves Tuchel with three full days before Wednesday's Group L opener against Croatia in Dallas. Replacing the standard training kit is straightforward.Match boots are not. Elite footballers typically train and play exclusively in personalised, broken-in footwear, and sourcing replacements at short notice for a full international squad presents a genuine operational headache.The FA is still assessing the full extent of the losses. England hold a community training session on Saturday afternoon before the squad convenes for Sunday's first full session. With Croatia waiting on Wednesday, Tuchel has no margin to absorb further disruption. The boots need replacing. The preparation needs to hold.
Article Summary: England's World Cup squad arrived in Kansas City on Saturday afternoon. Two subjects of interest have been taken into custody pending further investigation. The FA is still assessing the full extent of the losses. England open their 2026 FIFA World Cup Group L campaign against Croatia on Wednesday.

Article Title: Analyst spots a hidden meaning in Mike Bajakian's message amid Shedeur Sanders' QB battle
Article Text:
Analyst spots a hidden meaning in Mike Bajakian's message amid Shedeur Sanders' QB battle(Getty)

Veteran analyst Tod Grossi believes Mike Bajakian's message hints at what could decide Shedeur Sanders' fate

The Cleveland Browns insist the quarterback competition remains open. Yet one comment from a coach has sparked fresh debate around Shedeur Sanders' chances of winning the job.Veteran Cleveland Browns insider Tony Grossi believes the message behind the words matters just as much as the words themselves. His interpretation of a recent remark from quarterbacks coach Mike Bajakian has added another layer to an already fascinating battle heading into training camp.Speaking about the Cleveland Browns' quarterback situation this week, head coach Mike Bajakian said the starter would "declare itself pretty quickly once the pads come on." It sounded simple enough. Tony Grossi, however, heard something more.During his recent analysis, Tony Grossi suggested the Cleveland Browns already know what they want to see when training camp intensifies. "I'm reading into that, believing they think that Watson's ability to deliver the ball under a pass rush is clearly better than Shedeur right now," Grossi said. "And they're not going to go weeks into camp to make this decision. Watson's got 10 years' experience. I mean, he was a good quarterback at one point. .."Tony Grossi added that he noticed similar trends throughout spring practices. "I've seen it out there all spring.And Watson, too, has suffered from not processing quickly enough. And I just feel that they feel he's the superior athlete, and Monken wants an athlete."Plus, Mike Bajakian never named a favourite. Also, Tony Grossi never said the competition had ended. Still, his interpretation introduced a new wrinkle. Experience, pocket movement and handling pressure may carry more weight than many expected.For Shedeur Sanders, that changes the conversation. Reports from offseason workouts suggested the young quarterback had narrowed the gap. Browns offensive coordinator Todd Monken also said both players had "earned the right to compete to start," reinforcing that no final decision had arrived.Even so, training camp now feels less like a long audition and more like a short sprint. If Tony Grossi's read proves accurate, Shedeur Sanders may need more than flashes of promise. He may have to force the Browns to rethink what they already believe once the pads finally come on. For all the talk about an open competition, the margin for error appears razor-thin. Sanders now faces the challenge of changing perceptions before the Browns settle on a direction.
Article Summary: Cleveland Browns head coach Mike Bajakian said the starter would "declare itself pretty quickly once the pads come on" Veteran analyst Tony Grossi believes the message behind the words matters just as much as the words themselves. Grossi: "I'm reading into that, believing they think that Watson's ability to deliver the ball under a pass rush is clearly better than Shedeur right now"

Article Title: Dak Prescott and other NFL stars reveal their bold 2026 FIFA World Cup predictions that fans can't overlook
Article Text:
Dak Prescott and other NFL stars reveal their bold 2026 FIFA World Cup favorites that fans can't overlook(Getty)

France leads the race as Dak Prescott and other NFL players reveal their 2026 FIFA World Cup favourites

The NFL may be enjoying its summer break, but many of its biggest names already have another championship on their minds. As the 2026 FIFA World Cup captures attention across North America, several NFL stars have traded football talk for futbol debates.A survey of 29 NFL players has now revealed where their loyalties and predictions lie. While a few backed traditional powerhouses and others rooted for the host nation, one country received far more support than the rest.France has become the team to beat, at least in the eyes of NFL players. As per the recent survey by NFL Nation Reporters, Les Bleus emerged as the clear favourites to win the FIFA World Cup. The support stretched across locker rooms and positions, showing just how much faith NFL stars have in the depth and quality of the French squad.Not everyone made the same choice. Dallas Cowboys quarterback Dak Prescott admitted that his emotions and football instincts pointed in different directions. He wants the United States to make a memorable run on home soil. However, when asked to predict, Prescott picked Brazil to lift the trophy."Obviously, I'm very optimistic and hopeful," said Dak Prescott, quarterback for the Dallas Cowboys, "but if I didn't have to give the politically correct answer, I'd choose Brazil.”France, though, kept attracting votes. Marcus Mariota and Washington Commanders wide receiver Terry McLaurin threw their support behind the 2018 world champions.Mariota said, “On paper, France has the best team, but the World Cup is always interesting: it all depends on who plays well on the day."Much of that confidence centred around Kylian Mbappe. The France captain remains one of the most feared attackers in the game and enters the tournament in his prime years.The results made one thing obvious. France did not edge past the competition by a narrow margin. They dominated the conversation. There was also room for football romance. Kansas City Chiefs kicker Harrison Butker said he hoped to see Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi face each other one last time in the knockout rounds. For fans who have spent two decades watching the pair redefine greatness, it would be a fitting chapter in World Cup history.Meanwhile, belief in the United States remains strong despite the difficult road ahead. Several NFL players expressed hope that the host nation could produce its deepest run in generations. The US men's team has never progressed beyond the quarter-finals in the modern World Cup era.The survey offered an interesting glimpse into how NFL stars view the world's biggest sporting event. Patriotism influenced some choices. Admiration shaped others. Yet when prediction time arrived, the verdict felt decisive.France stood above the rest as the team NFL players believe has the best chance of becoming world champions.
Article Summary: Dak Prescott and other NFL stars reveal their bold 2026 FIFA World Cup favorites. France stands above the rest as the team NFL players believe has the best chance of becoming world champions. Several NFL players expressed hope that the host nation could produce its deepest run in generations.

Article Title: "She needs to publicly apologize to Vrabel": Dianna Russini controversy sparks heated reactions as Mike Vrabel apology demands surface online
Article Text:
NFL insider Dianna Russini is facing growing online scrutiny after comments from former player and analyst Mark Schlereth fueled speculation about a wider league concern. Tony Farmer highlighted Schlereth’s claim that “the entire league is nervous” about the reported investigation tied to Russini and Mike Vrabel rumors. Social media reactions quickly turned heated, with fans debating the seriousness of the controversy while others warned against jumping to conclusions without confirmed facts.

Dianna Russini investigation rumors spark wider NFL concerns

Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini controversy fuels social media backlash

Questions surrounding NFL insider Dianna Russini are gaining fresh attention online after a resurfaced video and a wave of social media reactions pulled veteran analyst Mark Schlereth into the conversation. While no official findings have emerged, speculation around a reported investigation has now spread far beyond Patriots circles.The latest debate exploded after sports commentator Tony Farmer shared a clip discussing Schlereth’s reaction to the growing controversy. The comments quickly sparked heated reactions from football fans, many of whom began wondering how deep the situation could reach inside league media circles.Tony Farmer raised eyebrows after connecting Schlereth’s long NFL background and his previous overlap with Russini during their ESPN years. In his video commentary, he said:“This is a good one here, guys. Remember that Mark Schleyerith worked at ESPN for two overlapping years with Diana Rossini. Schleyerith, three-time Super Bowl champion, doesn't have anything to do with the Diana Rossini-Mike Vrabel scandal, but he probably knows a lot of people who do have to do with it.""Schleyerith worked in the NFL from 1989 to 2000 as a player, and he worked with ESPN for a while, including those two years where he overlapped with Dianna Russini. And I asked him, how concerned, Mark, do you think the Patriots are about the athletics investigation into Dianna Russini's conduct? He took it a step further. I think the entire league is nervous.What do you think Mark Schleyerith meant by that? He thinks the entire league is nervous about this investigation. What could possibly come out of the Dianna Russini report or investigation or inquiry, whatever you want to call it, that could make the entire league nervous?”Those remarks quickly gained traction online because they hinted at concerns extending beyond a single franchise. Fans immediately began speculating about possible league-wide implications, though no verified evidence has publicly confirmed broader wrongdoing.The discussion intensified after several strong reactions appeared on X. One user, Ron Cossette, posted: “Is Dianna Russini that much of a whore? Good Lord. Also, she needs to publicly apologize to Mike Vrabel.”Another account responded directly underneath, writing, “Yes, yes she is.”The comments drew attention because they reflected how emotional and divided online reactions have become around the rumors involving Russini and Patriots coach Mike Vrabel. At the same time, many fans also pushed back against the harsh language, warning against rushing to judgment before verified facts emerge.So far, neither Russini nor the NFL has publicly addressed the growing online speculation. Still, the story continues gaining momentum as fans search for answers about what exactly could have the league “nervous.”
Article Summary: NFL insider Dianna Russini is facing growing online scrutiny. Comments from former player Mark Schlereth fueled speculation about a wider league concern. Social media reactions quickly turned heated, with fans debating the seriousness of the controversy. No official findings have emerged, but speculation has now spread far beyond Patriots circles.

Article Title: Matthew Stafford lost $1 million after selling Drake’s houses following two years on the market

Article Title: Taylor Swift’s biggest songwriting night takes a wild turn as Travis Kelce joins Steven Spielberg-filmed viral moment
Article Text:
Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce turned a Songwriters Hall of Fame celebration into a viral moment when Steven Spielberg filmed them dancing. (Image via Getty)

Taylor Swift handed Steven Spielberg her phone, and the internet got the cleanest celebrity flex of the night

Swift’s viral dance came after an emotional speech about the work behind the songs

Taylor Swift did not need a director for her Songwriters Hall of Fame moment. She got Steven Spielberg anyway.Swift was inducted into the Songwriters Hall of Fame on Thursday, June 11, at the Marriott Marquis in New York City. But after the speeches, the emotional family moment and the industry honors, one clip cut through the noise: Swift handing her phone to Spielberg while she danced with Travis Kelce.According to TMZ, Swift and Kelce were dancing while John Fogerty and his sons performed “Fortunate Son” at the ceremony. Swift then gave her phone to Spielberg, who was seated beside her, and let the Oscar-winning director film the moment.That is ridiculous in the best possible way. Most people ask a friend to record a dance video. Swift had the director of “Jaws,” “E.T.” and “Jurassic Park” doing it from the next chair.The clip worked because nobody in it looked confused about the assignment. Swift leaned into the bit. Kelce joined in. Spielberg did not treat it like a throwaway phone video. He filmed it like he understood the internet would do the rest.The moment also gave fans a rare public look at Swift and Kelce together after Kelce had been away with the Kansas City Chiefs for offseason work. TMZ reported Kelce made it to the event in time for Swift’s acceptance speech and sat with her at a table that included their mothers, Andrea Swift and Donna Kelce.That detail matters. Kelce was not just there for a viral dance clip. He was there for one of the biggest songwriting honors of Swift’s career.The dance clip was funny. The reason Swift was in the room was not small.The Songwriters Hall of Fame announced Swift as part of its 2026 class alongside Alanis Morissette, Kenny Loggins, Walter Afanasieff, Terry Britten, Graham Lyle, Christopher “Tricky” Stewart, Gene Simmons and Paul Stanley of KISS.Vogue reported that Swift became the youngest-ever female inductee at 36. The ceremony also featured Spielberg introducing her before she gave a roughly 20-minute acceptance speech.Swift teared up while speaking about her family and the sacrifices they made when she was young. Vogue reported that she talked about moving to Nashville and building a career that forced her to learn the entertainment business in public.She also made a clear distinction between the parts of fame she had to learn and the part that came naturally. “But songwriting for me was pretty much the only thing I naturally did,” Swift said, according to Vogue.That is the useful context behind the viral clip. The Spielberg video was not just a random celebrity party moment. It happened on a night built around Swift’s actual job: writing songs that moved from teenage diary entries to stadium setlists to Hall of Fame recognition.Kelce’s role in the night was simple but effective. He showed up, sat with her family and joined the fun when the room loosened up. Spielberg becoming the cameraman turned the clip into the headline, but Swift’s induction is the reason the moment had weight.The internet got its joke. Swift got her Hall of Fame honor. Kelce got another public boyfriend win without needing to say a word.
Article Summary: Swift was inducted into the Songwriters Hall of Fame on Thursday, June 11, at the Marriott Marquis in New York City. Swift and Travis Kelce were dancing while John Fogerty and his sons performed “Fortunate Son” Swift then gave her phone to Spielberg, who was seated beside her, and let the Oscar-winning director film the moment.

Article Title: Seattle Seahawks’ Super Bowl Rings: Inside massive diamond studded bling that hides personalized message and common trait
Article Text:
Seattle Seahawks’ Super Bowl Rings: Inside massive diamond studded bling that hides personalized secret and common trait (Image via X: Seattle Seahawks Super Bowl LX ring)

Inside personalized Seattle Seahawks Super Bowl rings that boast WORLD CHAMPIONS in style

Seahawks’ Super Bowl ring features

Super Bowl LX ring special traits

Jason Arasheben, founder of Jason of Beverly Hills, spoke about the Seahawks ring

Jason of Beverly Hills designed Super Bowl LX rings for the Seattle Seahawks players.Two features set it apart from other rings: Player Side and Special Button.The “player side” is unique for every player:One side of the ring is personalized with the athlete’s last name, number, and the team mantra [M.O.B.].M.O.B. stands for Mission Over B*llSh*t.The other side, or the “team side,” features the franchise’s vision: “12 AS ONE” alongside the Seattle skyline, the Championship year, and the Super Bowl LX logo.Now, the “special button.”The ring has been engineered with a special button that releases and expands the famous Lumen Field arches to reveal a secret message for the player:“WORLD CHAMPIONS.”This will be a constant reminder to them that they are the best! They were the best in the 2025 season… They won a Super Bowl. And that they can achieve more…The “ring top” features a centerpiece showcasing blue sapphires, the Seahawks logo, and two Lombardi Trophies, signifying two Super Bowl championships: 2013 and 2025.One [2013], in which Russell Wilson [now a CBS analyst] was the quarterback.And second [2025], where Sam Darnold [considered a “bust” by everyone] was the quarterback.The “Seahawks logo” comprises 50 brilliant round white diamonds surrounding the center logo in tribute to the organization’s 50th season.The “Lumen Field arches” were inspired by Lumen Field’s architecture. The stadium’s iconic arches take center stage.They are crafted in white gold and accented with prong-set baguette diamonds.The “diamonds” are round, emerald-cut, marquise-cut white diamonds.They are carefully set to amplify brilliance and celebrate the team’s championship achievement.The bottom of the ring features 12 feathers in tribute to the team’s iconic fanbase, the “12s.”The inside of the ring reads, “17 WINS” to represent the team’s combined championship wins of the season.The top of the ring is detachable from its base, allowing it to be worn as a pendant.The inside of the ring features an authentic piece of a football used during the season, with the number “50“ in the middle to represent the franchise’s 50th season.“This is an incredible honor for our company,” said Jason Arasheben via NFL Network. “Very few manufacturers in the United States are considered for opportunities like this.So being selected by the Seahawks is deeply meaningful. From the beginning, the goal was never simply to create another championship ring.We created a lasting symbol of one of the most important moments in Seahawks history.A ring that captured the identity of the organization, the resilience of the season, and the passion of the fanbase behind it.”
Article Summary: Jason of Beverly Hills designed Super Bowl LX rings for the Seattle Seahawks players. Ring has been engineered with a special button that releases and expands the famous Lumen Field arches to reveal a secret message for the player. Ring features an authentic piece of a football used during the season, with the number “50” in the middle to represent the franchise’s 50th season.

Article Title: Travis Kelce’s Kansas City Chiefs assistant Dave Merritt avoids NFL discipline after domestic battery case falls apart
Article Text:
Kansas City Chiefs defensive backs coach Dave Merritt will not face NFL discipline after his domestic battery case was dismissed. (Image via Getty)

Why the NFL is not disciplining Kansas City Chiefs assistant Dave Merritt



Dave Merritt remains with the Chiefs after a case that could have followed him into the season



Kansas City Chiefs assistant Dave Merritt will not face discipline from the NFL after a domestic battery case against him was dismissed in May.The decision closes the league side of a case that began in April, when Merritt was charged after being accused of causing bodily harm to his daughter. Merritt pleaded not guilty, the case was later dismissed, and the NFL has now reportedly found insufficient evidence that he violated its personal conduct policy.The NFL can discipline coaches and players even when criminal cases do not lead to convictions. That did not happen here. According to Mike Garafolo of NFL Network, the league found insufficient evidence of a personal conduct policy violation. That finding allows Merritt to continue his work with Kansas City without a suspension or other league punishment.Merritt was charged in April with misdemeanor domestic battery. The charge came after an allegation that he caused bodily harm to his daughter. He pleaded not guilty, and the case was dismissed in May.The dismissal mattered, but it did not automatically end the NFL’s review. The league has its own personal conduct policy process, and it can act even when the legal system does not move forward. In Merritt’s case, the NFL reviewed the matter and did not find enough evidence to impose discipline.That distinction is important. Merritt was not suspended because the league reportedly did not find sufficient evidence under its policy. The case being dismissed in court was one step. The NFL’s decision was another.For Kansas City, the result removes a potential staff issue before the season. Merritt has been the Chiefs’ defensive backs coach since 2019 and remains part of one of the NFL’s most stable coaching staffs.Merritt’s football résumé is not small, which is part of why the case drew attention. The 54-year-old has coached defensive backs for the Chiefs since 2019. Before Kansas City, he worked for the Arizona Cardinals, New York Giants, and New York Jets. He also spent time as a college assistant coach.Merritt entered coaching after a brief NFL playing career. The Miami Dolphins selected him in the seventh round of the 1993 NFL Draft, and he later moved into coaching after his playing days ended. The timing of the charge also put the Chiefs in a difficult spot. It came in April, during the NFL Draft window, when teams are building rosters and preparing rookies for offseason work. Kansas City still had to let the legal process play out before the league reached its own conclusion.Now, Merritt is free to resume coaching without NFL discipline attached to the case. That does not erase why the situation received attention. Domestic battery allegations are serious, and the NFL has disciplined personnel in past cases even when criminal proceedings did not result in charges or convictions. But in this case, the court case was dismissed in May, and the NFL reportedly determined there was not enough evidence to punish Merritt under the personal conduct policy.For the Chiefs, that means one less unresolved issue around the coaching staff. For Merritt, it means his legal case and league review are both behind him as Kansas City moves forward.
Article Summary: Dave Merritt was charged in April with misdemeanor domestic battery. The charge came after an allegation that he caused bodily harm to his daughter. He pleaded not guilty, and the case was later dismissed. The NFL has now reportedly found insufficient evidence that he violated its personal conduct policy.

Article Title: Dak Prescott jokes breakup stress with Sarah Jane Ramos helped him shed pounds before 2026 NFL season
Article Text:
Dak Prescott jokes about weight loss, says "stress may help lose a little weight here and there" (Getty Images)

Why did Dak Prescott joke about stress causing his weight loss?



What led to Dak Prescott and Sarah Jane Ramos breaking off their engagement?



Dallas Cowboys quarterback Dak Prescott made his most candid public comment yet about life after his split with fiancée Sarah Jane Ramos. Speaking to reporters on June 9 during a Cowboys media session ahead of minicamp, Prescott raised eyebrows when he acknowledged looking noticeably thinner. He smiled through it, cracked a joke about stress, and shut down any follow-up before it got personal. But the moment still said something.Several reporters attending the Cowboys minicamp session noticed Prescott looked leaner than he did during the 2025 season. When asked about his physical condition, he deflected with humour."Stress may help lose a little weight here and there," he said, grinning.The room picked up on what he left unsaid. When a reporter pushed further and asked whether the stress was tied to his breakup with Sarah Jane Ramos, Prescott laughed it off immediately, said he was only joking, and moved the conversation along. It was brief, controlled, and deliberate. For someone who has stayed almost completely silent about the split since March, even a half-joke felt significant.Off the field, Prescott and Ramos appear to be handling things with more maturity than the tabloids gave them credit for. Despite the messy circumstances surrounding their breakup, the two reached a temporary custody agreement in April 2026. Their legal teams put out statements that were notably civil.Dak's attorney called it a positive step forward. Sarah's team said she was relieved the two could make decisions without a drawn-out court battle. The former couple share two daughters: Margaret Jane, known as MJ, and Aurora. They recently came together to celebrate Aurora's first birthday, a quiet signal that co-parenting is the priority now.The couple ended their engagement in March 2026, just weeks before a planned wedding in Italy. Dak and Sarah had been together since 2023, went public later that year, and announced a pregnancy in November 2023. MJ arrived in March 2024, followed by a proposal in October 2024, a second daughter Aurora in 2025, and what looked like a fairytale heading into their wedding year.Then it fell apart. Reports pointed to a joint bachelor-bachelorette trip to the Bahamas as a turning point. Multiple outlets claimed Sarah discovered inappropriate messages between Prescott and women from his past. There were also allegations involving secret social media accounts and ongoing contact with former relationships. Neither Prescott nor Ramos has publicly confirmed or denied any of it.Some reports later threw in claims about financial disagreements and prenuptial tensions. Sarah's representatives pushed back on those directly, stating that money played no role in the separation.
Article Summary: Dak Prescott made his most candid public comment yet about his split with fiancée Sarah Jane Ramos. He acknowledged looking noticeably thinner during a Cowboys media session ahead of minicamp. When asked about his physical condition, he deflected with humour."Stress may help lose a little weight here and there"

Article Title: Dianna Russini-Mike Vrabel scandal: 3-time Super Bowl champion reveals why every team in the NFL should be worried right now
Article Text:
Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel (Getty Images)

3-time Super Bowl champion warns the NFL isn't done with the Vrabel-Russini scandal and explains why



Two months in and The Athletic's investigation into Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini still has no finish line. The mandatory minicamp opened in Foxborough on June 9, and the unfinished review continues to cast a shadow over an offseason that the Patriots would rather forget. Three-time Super Bowl champion Mark Schlereth put it plainly when asked about the situation on X: "I think the entire league is nervous." That one line says more about the gravity of this moment than any press release could.The Athletic's senior editor Steven Ginsberg held an all-hands meeting last week and told staffers the review is going to take a few more weeks, noting there is simply a lot to go through. That timeline, vague as it is, has done nothing to calm speculation. If anything, it has kept the pressure building at a steady, uncomfortable pace.The concern for the Patriots goes beyond optics. The review is intended to address questions about Dianna Russini's impartiality across years of NFL reporting and how the culture at The Athletic fits within the Times newsroom that acquired it in 2022, with one insider calling the episode embarrassing for staff. That is the editorial side. On the football side, the franchise faces a different kind of reckoning: what does it do if the findings are damaging enough to make Vrabel a liability?There have already been rumors that Robert Kraft and the higher-ups in New England have devised a succession plan, with some suggesting Mike Tomlin is at the top of their list of replacements.The hope is it never reaches that point, but it signals how seriously the organization is treating what could come next.Vrabel himself told reporters in April that he had "difficult conversations with people I care about," including his family, coaching staff, team officials and players, and that those conversations had been positive and productive. He also left the door open to stepping away again, a detail that has not gone unnoticed. Vrabel stepped away during the draft for counselling and family, then turned up in Salt Lake City the same weekend, sharpening media scrutiny rather than quieting it, before resurfacing when OTAs opened on May 27.Russini's path looks murkier. She resigned from The Athletic on her own terms shortly after the story broke, stating, "This media frenzy is hurtling forward without regard for the review process The Athletic is trying to complete. I do so not because I accept the narrative that has been constructed around this episode, but because I refuse to lend it further oxygen or to let it define me or my career." Tony Farmer, who has tracked the story closely, remains sceptical about her return to reporting: "It's possible. America is a forgiving country when people own their mistakes. But when she deleted her Twitter, to me, that was a signal that she was closing that door."For now, the Patriots push through minicamp. Vrabel coaches. The investigation grinds on. And the rest of the league watches.
Article Summary: The Athletic's investigation into Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini still has no finish line. Three-time Super Bowl champion Mark Schlereth: "I think the entire league is nervous" The review is intended to address questions about Russini's impartiality across years of NFL reporting.

Article Title: Former Kansas City Chiefs champion faces the most serious charge of his life amid ongoing murder case
Article Text:
Former Kansas City Chiefs champion faces the most serious charge of his life in ongoing murder case( Getty)

Prosecutors push ahead with first-degree murder charge against former NFL star

The football world has seen plenty of shocking headlines over the years. Few, however, match the stunning turn in the life of former NFL star Darron Lee. Once part of the Kansas City Chiefs' Super Bowl-winning roster, Lee now finds himself at the centre of a murder case that could change the course of his life forever.A grand jury in Tennessee has indicted the former Kansas City Chiefs linebacker on a first-degree murder charge in connection with the death of his girlfriend, Gabriella Carvalho Perpetuo. The case has moved beyond an investigation. It now heads towards what could become one of the most closely watched legal battles involving a former NFL player in recent years.A Hamilton County grand jury returned an indictment charging Darron Lee with first-degree murder. Prosecutors later dropped a separate tampering with evidence charge to focus entirely on securing a murder conviction.Authorities accuse Lee of killing Perpetuo at a residence in Ooltewah, Tennessee, in February 2026. According to reports presented in court, an autopsy found that the 29-year-old suffered multiple blunt force injuries. The report listed several injuries, including hematomas, fractures and stab wounds.One detail from the case quickly drew national attention. Prosecutors alleged that Lee used ChatGPT while dealing with the situation. Court proceedings revealed that investigators found messages in which he allegedly asked how to get medical help without contacting police. Prosecutors also said he asked whether a fall could explain bruising around both eyes and what were described as "two stabby looking wounds. "Lee remains in custody at the Hamilton County Jail without bond. Prosecutors have not ruled out seeking the death penalty. A final decision on that issue could come in the coming weeks.The case marks a dramatic fall for a player once viewed as one of college football's brightest defensive talents. Lee starred at Ohio State and helped the Buckeyes win a national championship before the New York Jets selected him with the 20th overall pick in the 2016 NFL Draft.He later joined the Kansas City Chiefs during their 2019 Super Bowl season and earned a championship ring, even though he did not play in the title game. His final NFL appearance came with the Buffalo Bills in November 2020.For now, the football story has faded into the background. The focus rests on the courtroom, where Lee faces the most serious charge of his life. He has pleaded not guilty, and under the law, remains presumed innocent unless proven guilty at trial.
Article Summary: Prosecutors push ahead with first-degree murder charge against former NFL star Darron Lee. Former Kansas City Chiefs champion faces the most serious charge of his life in ongoing murder case. Prosecutors have not ruled out seeking the death penalty. Prosecutors alleged that Lee used ChatGPT while dealing with the situation.

Article Title: Travis Kelce is days away from marrying Taylor Swift and his coach just dropped a bombshell response
Article Text:
Andy Reid won't say if he's invited to Travis Kelce's wedding but his response reveals everything (Getty Images)

Is Travis Kelce distracted by his wedding with Taylor Swift?



Did Travis Kelce skip Taylor Swift's NBA Finals appearance for Chiefs practice?



Kansas City Chiefs head coach Andy Reid addressed the elephant in the room on Thursday, June 11, as reporters pressed him on whether Travis Kelce's upcoming wedding to Taylor Swift was becoming a distraction during mandatory minicamp. Reid was brief, borderline secretive about the wedding itself, but had nothing but praise for his tight end's work on the field. With the 2026-27 NFL season approaching, Kelce is now entering his 14th year in the league and by all accounts, his head is firmly in the game.Short answer from Andy Reid: no.When a reporter asked the 68-year-old coach about Kelce's progress and whether he planned to attend the wedding, Reid shut down the personal side of the conversation almost instantly. "Can't talk about it," he said. "Can't talk about it." He didn't elaborate further -- and clearly had no intention to.But when the topic shifted to football, Reid was notably warmer. "Listen, he's been here most of the offseason, if not the whole offseason," the coach said. "He's been around and it's good to have him here." He added that Kelce completed the mandatory camp with strong effort: "He did the mandatory camp and did a nice job there so it's good to have him back in and rolling.He's very excited. You see no distractions with that and the wedding. "For a player marrying one of the most famous people on the planet, that kind of feedback from his coach carries weight. Kelce, who recently signed a new deal to remain in Kansas City, is not exactly operating in a low-pressure environment. The Chiefs had an average run last season by their standards, and expectations this year are predictably high. The last thing the team needs is their star tight end mentally checked out during the summer. Reid's comments suggest that is simply not the case.Yes, and it was not a one-off.While Taylor Swift made headlines at Madison Square Garden during Game 4 of the NBA Finals, attending the New York Knicks' dramatic comeback win over the San Antonio Spurs with friends Alana and Este Haim, Kelce was nowhere near the venue. The Chiefs' official Instagram later explained why -- a photo from their mandatory minicamp showed Kelce mid-session, meeting his NFL obligations.It was the same situation when Swift attended the Toy Story 5 premiere. Kelce skipped that too, for the same reason.The pattern here is telling. Wedding buzz or not, Kelce has been consistently showing up to put in the work while Swift handles the high-profile social calendar. Reid himself seemed almost amused by the dynamic when speaking on 96.5 The Fan in May. "If it's like when I got married, my wife did everything so I just kind of followed her lead on it and showed up, right?" he said. Then, with a grin in his voice: "Maybe he's doing more, but he looks like he's pretty focused in on this job here, too. "Reid also teased, during that same radio appearance, that he likely has a wedding invitation waiting for him -- and that he plans to attend if his tuxedo still fits.
Article Summary: Andy Reid won't say if he's invited to Travis Kelce's wedding but his response reveals everything. Reid was brief, borderline secretive about the wedding itself, but had nothing but praise for his tight end's work on the field. Reid himself seemed almost amused by the dynamic when speaking on 96.5 The Fan in May.

San Antonio Spurs reserve center Luke Kornet is listed as questionable with illness ahead of Game 5 of the 2025 NBA Finals on June 13 at Frost Bank Center (7:30 p.m. CT), with the Spurs trailing the New York Knicks 3-1. Victor Wembanyama, 22, played 44 minutes in Game 4, scoring 24 points and 13 rebounds while shooting 9-of-25. The Knicks erased a 29-point deficit to win 107-106 via OG Anunoby's tip-in with 1.2 seconds remaining, the largest comeback in NBA Finals history.Luke Kornet is listed as questionable with illness and has not been a factor offensively in the Finals, averaging just under eight minutes per game, totalling three points and nine rebounds across the series. The blunt reality is that Kornet's statistical contribution barely registers. The problem is structural: he is the only available big man who gives Mitch Johnson a nominal alternative to playing Wembanyama into the ground.Kelly Olynyk, Bismack Biyombo, and Mason Plumlee represent a clear drop-off from an already-limited option. Johnson's most likely adjustment is going small, with Carter Bryant absorbing center minutes, a role Bryant has filled sparingly and unconvincingly this postseason.In Game 4, Wembanyama played 44 minutes, five fewer than he logged across two overtime periods against Oklahoma City in the Western Conference Finals, and the fatigue was visible. He scored eight points after halftime on 3-of-14 shooting and missed two free throws with 1:47 remaining, turnovers the Knicks converted into the foundation of a 29-point comeback capped by OG Anunoby's tip-in with 1.2 seconds left.Johnson acknowledged the workload was a calculated risk. "He had a few more minutes tonight because we were trying to put the game away," the coach said. "With two days after this, what was at stake, we wanted to win the game."The Spurs did not use any reserve big man in the second half as New York surged. With Kornet potentially unavailable, Karl-Anthony Towns and Mitchell Robinson will get an even cleaner path to the glass. Wembanyama knows what the moment requires. "The bad one would be giving up," he said after the loss. "The good one would be getting stronger through this, getting more together. I know this is what we're going to do."Game 5 tips off Saturday at 7:30 p.m. CT at Frost Bank Center. The Knicks, one win from their first NBA title since 1973, arrive carrying the largest comeback in Finals history.
Article Summary: San Antonio Spurs reserve center Luke Kornet is listed as questionable with illness ahead of Game 5 of the 2025 NBA Finals on June 13 at Frost Bank Center (7:30 p.m. CT) The Knicks erased a 29-point deficit to win 107-106 via OG Anunoby's tip-in with 1.2 seconds remaining. Victor Wembanyama played 44 minutes in Game 4, scoring 24 points and 13 rebounds while shooting 9-of-25.

Article Title: Scooter Braun reveals why he and Sydney Sweeney sat behind Taylor Swift at the NBA finals
Article Text:
Image - X

Sydney Sweeney surprised Scooter Braun with family tickets to the Knicks finals



"She surprised me with tickets in the same section with my pops and my brother so I could share it with them,"

"Let's keep things positive and get One more!"

"one of the greatest nights of my life"

"I don't want to hear any negativity. To be able to share it with my dad and my brother is something I'll never forget,"

"lifetime Knicks fans."

"the best game ever"

Sydney Sweeney deserved better from this news cycle



When photos emerged of Scooter Braun sitting several rows behind Taylor Swift at Game 4 of the NBA Finals, the internet did what it always does: it found the most dramatic explanation and ran with it. The narrative wrote itself: music's most infamous enemies, inches apart at Madison Square Garden, seven years after the master's feud that defined both of their public lives. Except the actual story had nothing to do with any of that.Braun explained it himself, Sydney Sweeney had surprised him with tickets in the same section as his father, Ervin, and his brother Adam, so they could all watch together.he wrote.He wasn't bumped from celebrity row. He chose family over it.Braun posted about the night at length, saying it wasand that he hadn't slept.he wrote, describing them asHe also thanked Sweeney specifically for the gesture, and the couple was photographed kissing after the historic win.Sweeney, for her part, called iton her Instagram Stories. She showed up in Knicks gear and heels, packed on the PDA, and watched her boyfriend celebrate with his family after a 29-point comeback win. That's the story. It's a good one.Instead, the discourse zeroed in on where Braun was sitting relative to Swift, resurrecting a feud that both parties have publicly tried to move past. Braun said on the Second Thought podcast just weeks earlier that he had "never had a substantial conversation" with Swift and didn't fully understand how he became the villain of the story. Swift bought back her masters last year. Braun said, "I am happy for her." They are not friends, but they are also not enemies actively feuding in 2026.Sweeney did something thoughtful, she coordinated tickets so her boyfriend could experience one of the biggest sports nights in New York history with his family. The result was dismissed as a celebrity-row snub and a footnote in someone else's feud.Braun looked "dazed" walking out, according to TMZ, which was immediately read as Taylor-adjacent anxiety. The man had just watched the Knicks erase a 29-point deficit in the NBA Finals with his father and brother. He was probably just overwhelmed.The Swiftie internet is powerful, fast, and rarely wrong about matters that directly concern Taylor Swift. This one didn't. Scooter Braun sat a few rows back because his girlfriend planned a family night. That's it.
Article Summary: Sydney Sweeney surprised Scooter Braun with family tickets to the Knicks finals. He sat a few rows back because his girlfriend planned a family night. The result was dismissed as a celebrity-row snub and a footnote in someone else's feud.

Article Title: Rihanna airballing with fans, Timothée going shirtless, Taylor Swift celebrating courtside: Inside the wild Knicks night
Article Text:
Image - X

Inside MSG: the most famous building in New York



Timothée Chalamet went fully feral



"Beautiful."

Taylor Swift and the custom tee squad



"I love you so much. I'm so happy."

Rihanna and A$AP Rocky took it to the streets



And then NYC went feral



The New York Knicks came back from 29 points down to win Game 4 of the NBA Finals 107-106 yesterday. And the celebrities inside Madison Square Garden lost every last bit of composure they had.This wasn't a normal celebrity row night. This was a full-scale A-list meltdown, the good kind- spilling out of MSG, onto 7th Avenue, and into the streets of Manhattan in scenes nobody expected to be documenting the next morning.The Game 4 celebrity lineup included Spike Lee, Ben Stiller, Tracy Morgan , Adam Sandler, Fat Joe, and Chris Rock, the Knicks faithful who show up every single time. Michael J. Fox and his wife Tracy Pollan were there, alongside Nas. Sydney Sweeney brought Scooter Braun. Taylor Swift sat courtside in her "Stevie Knicks" tee with the Haim sisters and Mariska Hargitay. Wu-Tang Clan performed at halftime, a full reunion, Staten Island representing on one of the biggest nights in Knicks history.And then OG Anunoby tipped in the game-winner with one second left. The building detonated.Chalamet led the postgame chaos inside MSG, dancing through the hallways with fans, including Ben Stiller, before ripping his shirt off entirely while Kylie Jenner filmed the whole thing. When a reporter caught him and asked how he was feeling, he looked straight into the camera and said one word:That's it. That was the quote. New York in a word.Swift danced with Este and Alana Haim in their matching custom shirts, "Stevie Knicks," "Knickleback," after the win, then hugged Kylie Jenner in the tunnel. Mariska Hargitay found Jalen Brunson and told him:Nobody was keeping it cool. Nobody was trying to.While celebrities were still losing it inside MSG, Rihanna and A$AP Rocky ended up on the streets of Manhattan shooting hoops with Knicks fans and missing. Rihanna, who rarely does anything publicly without making it a moment, was just out there airballing with regular New Yorkers at midnight. Iconic.As many as 10,000 fans flooded the streets around MSG, setting off fireworks, blocking traffic, climbing scaffolding, and attempting to flip a taxi. Police took 56 people into custody, and 10 officers were injured. The city had already cancelled the official outdoor watch party before the game after Game 3 celebrations led to 21 arrests, but it didn't matter. New York doesn't need a permit to riot with joy.Chuck D from Public Enemy was so moved by the win that he painted OG Anunoby's game-winning bucket. Iman Shumpert put on his old Knicks jersey and walked out onto 7th Avenue to celebrate with fans. A Baltimore Ravens quarterback was somewhere in Manhattan taking selfies. And somehow, Mumford & Sons and Tate McRae ended up celebrating the Knicks' victory together inside MSG, a sentence that should not exist but does.The Knicks are one win away from their first championship since 1973. If they close it out, whatever happened on June 10 will look like a rehearsal.
Article Summary: The New York Knicks came back from 29 points down to win Game 4 of the NBA Finals 107-106 yesterday. The celebrities inside Madison Square Garden lost every last bit of composure they had. This wasn't a normal celebrity row night. This was a full-scale A-list meltdown, the good kind.
